contradiction_text: AGENTS.md files show recurring topic categories, but the paper also reports that they do not converge on a consistent overall structure.
related_claim_texts: AGENTS.md files did not converge on a consistent structure, even though conventions or best practices, contribution guidance, and architecture or project structure were the most common heading categories.
disclosure: Repeated heading themes must stay visible alongside the limit that the analyzed AGENTS.md files still varied widely in content and presentation, so recurring topics should not be mistaken for a shared template or settled structure.

contradiction_text: The broader AGENTS.md set appears mostly stable or lightly maintained, while the high-change subset shows repeated instruction additions and modifications.
related_claim_texts: Most AGENTS.md files in the analyzed set were either never revised after creation or changed only once, while the high-change subset was dominated by added and modified instructions.
disclosure: The split between mostly unchanged files and the actively edited minority must remain visible, and the draft should keep clear that the detailed change-category evidence comes from only 10 high-change files rather than the full population.

contradiction_text: Adoption is spread across multiple languages, but overall visible uptake of the examined file formats remains low at 5% of the scanned sample.
related_claim_texts: Among repositories that used an AI configuration file, TypeScript repositories were the largest language group, while adoption was spread across several other languages at lower counts.
Only 466 of the 10,000 scanned repositories, or 5%, contained at least one of the four AI configuration file formats examined.
disclosure: Cross-language spread should stay visible, but it must not be smoothed into a maturity claim because the prevalence result is still sparse and is bounded to four scanned file types.
